items.title: 群聊时openclaw无法获取聊天人身份
items.content:
群聊中`@openclaw`助手聊天时，openclaw获取到的inbound元数据只有
```json
{
  "schema": "openclaw.inbound_meta.v1",
  "chat_id": "wecom:xxxxxxxxmaybegroupidxxxxxxxxx",
  "account_id": "default",
  "channel": "wecom",
  "provider": "wecom",
  "surface": "wecom",
  "chat_type": "group"
}
```
因而无法识别聊天人的身份

items.title: WhatsApp bridge: assistant output can leak into user messages during /new or /reset session transition
items.content:
## Summary

During a `/new` or `/reset` session transition, assistant-generated text can appear inside the next user inbound message. The user did not type this text, but the gateway logs it as a legitimate inbound message with `fromMe=false`.

## Impact

- **Content injection into user messages**: AI-generated content is attributed to the user
- **Loss of message integrity**: The system cannot distinguish between user-authored and assistant-authored text in the affected message
- **Potential for unintended actions**: The injected text may cause the new session to act on goals or framing the user never expressed

## Reproduction scenario

1. User is in an active session where the assistant is composing or has recently composed a reply
2. User sends `/new` or `/reset` to start a fresh session
3. User immediately sends their first message to the new session
4. The first message in the new session contains text the user did not write, concatenated with their actual message
5. Gateway logs show a single inbound message with the combined character count
6. `fromMe` flag is `false` on the combined message (incorrectly — part of it originated from the assistant)

## Observed behavior

- A single inbound WhatsApp message logged by the gateway contained ~840 characters of assistant-generated content prepended to the user's actual ~390 character message
- The assistant-generated content was stylistically identifiable as AI output (framing/purpose language), not user-written text
- The gateway logged this as one inbound message with correct sender ID but incorrect attribution
- No outbound messages were logged in the 20 minutes preceding the incident, suggesting the injected content was from a pending/buffered reply that lost its outbound attribution during the session transition

## Suspected root cause

Race condition in the WhatsApp plugin during session reset:

1. Assistant has a pending or partially-delivered reply in the outbound queue
2. `/new` fires, triggering session

items.title: False positive: jobautopilot-submitter flagged as suspicious by VirusTotal Code Insight
items.content:
**Title:** False positive: jobautopilot-submitter flagged as suspicious by VirusTotal Code Insight

**Skill:** `jerronl/jobautopilot-submitter`
**ClawHub page:** https://clawhub.ai/jerronl/jobautopilot-submitter

## What the skill does

This skill automates job application form submission. It:
- Reads the user's local tracker file and tailored resume/cover letter (their own files)
- Uses the browser tool to open job application pages
- Generates shell scripts in `/tmp` to fill form fields and upload documents
- Verifies successful submission and updates the local tracker

## Why the flag is a false positive

The `/tmp` script generation pattern is flagged as suspicious, but this is an intentional architectural choice documented in the SKILL.md: all page-state-changing browser actions must go through generated scripts rather than direct model calls, to prevent accidental state changes. The scripts contain only `openclaw browser fill/type/select/upload` commands targeting the currently open job application page — no system access, no external endpoints, no credential exfiltration.

All helper scripts (`check_required_fields.js`, `fill_template.sh`, `match_variant_options.sh`) are included in the skill package and publicly auditable at: https://github.com/jerronl/jobautopilot

The skill uses a dedicated isolated browser profile (`apply`) that is separate from the user's main browser. Password handling uses a user-supplied prefix stored in their local config — no passwords are hardcoded in the skill.

Please review and clear the flag.

**Author:** @jerronl

**Title:** False positive: jobautopilot-search flagged as suspicious by VirusTotal Code Insight

**Skill:** `jerronl/jobautopilot-search`
**ClawHub page:** https://clawhub.ai/jerronl/jobautopilot-search

## What the skill does

This is an instruction-only skill (no code files). It instructs an OpenClaw agent to:
- Read the user's local resume folder to build a candidate profile
- Search public job boards (LinkedIn, Indeed, Glassdoor, ZipRecruiter, Google Jobs) using the browser tool
- Filter results and write them to a local markdown tracker file

All file access is to the user's own local files. All web access is to public job listing pages. No credentials, API keys, or external services are involved.

## Why the flag is a false positive

The skill uses `browser: true` and a named browser profile (`search`) — both are standard OpenClaw patterns for any skill that needs web access. Reading local resume files is necessary for the stated purpose and is proportionate. The full source is public at: https://github.com/jerronl/jobautopilot

Please review and clear the flag.

**Author:** @jerronl

items.title: [Bug] WebChat: Message text covered by action icons after upgrade to 2026.3.31
items.content:
## Bug Description
After upgrading to 2026.3.31, the WebChat UI shows a layout issue where the action icons (copy/expand buttons) that should only appear on hover are permanently displayed and covering the end of message text.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open WebChat interface (http://127.0.0.1:18789)
2. Send any message
3. Look at sent messages - the copy/expand icon buttons are overlaid on top of message text

## Expected Behavior
Action icons should only appear on mouse hover over the message bubble.

## Actual Behavior
Action icons are fixed and visible, covering message content. Short messages get truncated - e.g. '我调整一下' displays as '我调整一'.

## Environment
- OpenClaw version: 2026.3.31
- OS: Windows

## Screenshot
[screenshot attached]

## Possible Cause
CSS z-index or positioning issue with the action button component in the webchat frontend.
items.author: warpig2023

items.title: v2026.3.31 npm package appears to miss runtime deps for bundled plugins (Telegram/Bedrock load failures)
items.content:
## Summary
`openclaw@2026.3.31` appears to ship a package/runtime layout where bundled plugins are loadable from `dist/extensions/*`, but required runtime dependencies for at least some plugins are not present in the published npm package metadata/tarball.

On Android/OpenClaw-Module, updating from stable `2026.3.28` to `2026.3.31` caused plugin load failures immediately after startup. Rolling back to `2026.3.28` restored normal behavior.

## First failure from runtime log
```text
[gateway] [plugins] telegram failed to load from /data/adb/openclaw/node/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/telegram/index.js: Error: Cannot find module 'grammy'
Require stack:
- /data/adb/openclaw/node/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/sticker-cache-1Tf7t-45.js
```

Also observed:
```text
[plugins] amazon-bedrock failed to load ... Error: Cannot find module '@aws-sdk/client-bedrock'
```

## What I checked
### 1) Published package metadata
`npm view openclaw@2026.3.31 version dependencies optionalDependencies bundledDependencies --json`

Observed from the published package / packed tarball:
- `dependencies`: no `grammy`
- `dependencies`: no `@aws-sdk/client-bedrock`
- `optionalDependencies`: no `grammy`
- `optionalDependencies`: no `@aws-sdk/client-bedrock`
- no `bundleDependencies` / `bundledDependencies`

### 2) Packed npm tarball
- `npm pack openclaw@2026.3.31`
- unpacked tarball and inspected `package/package.json`
- grep over unpacked package tree for:
  - `grammy`
  - `@aws-sdk/client-bedrock`

Result:
- no matching dependency declarations
- no direct refs in the packed tree from the audit grep
- `dist/extensions/telegram/index.js` is still present and exports the Telegram plugin entrypoint

## Why this looks like an upstream packaging/build issue
This does **not** look like a local updater-only problem:
- the failure appeared immediately on the updated runtime
- rollback to `2026.3.28` restored normal behavior
- the published npm package for `2026.3.31` itself appears not to car
items.author: thoitiettxl-cyber

items.title: feat(ops): reorganize Health/Diagnostics panels, merge Observability, remove Cron Health
items.content:
### Motivation
- Align the Operations (Health / Diagnostics) UI with the new information architecture by moving panels to the correct views, merging related observability sections, and removing a redundant Cron Health panel while preserving existing JS renderers and polling behaviour.
- Surface only external service dependencies in Health to avoid duplication with local Service Controls and reduce noise.

### Description
- Edited only `ops.html` to remove the Cron Health `<section>` from the Health view while keeping the `renderCronHealthCards()` function (it retains a null-check at the top to avoid errors when the element is absent). 
- Moved the Service Health panel from the Diagnostics view into Health and renamed it to **External Dependencies**, changing its subtitle and filtering rendered services to exclude local/control-plane entries (`provider === 'Local'` and IDs `openclaw`, `clawd_control`, `clawd-control`).
- Replaced the standalone Monitoring Layers panel in Health with a merged **Observability Health** panel that contains two labeled groups: "MONITORING STACK" (preserves `opsMonitoringLayersStale` and `opsMonitoringLayersCards`) and "DATA PIPELINE" (preserves `opsLogsHealthStale` and `opsLogsHealthGrid`), and added a small CSS rule `.ops-observability-group-label` for the group labels.
- Reordered the Diagnostics (`ops-checks-view`) HTML sections to the requested sequence: System Doctor, Deployment Verification, Bootstrap Context and Workspace Health, Version Check, and adjusted the sub-nav/view-switch logic and init timers so `loadServiceHealth()` and `loadOpsLogsHealth()` are triggered when the Health view is active and polling still runs from init.

### Testing
- Ran a Python structural check that inspected `ops.html` to confirm Health now contains: Infrastructure Health → Service Controls → External Dependencies → Observability Health → Gateway Events, and Diagnostics contains: System Doctor → Deployment Verification → Bootstrap Context and Workspac

items.title: OpenClaw + Ollama integration — wire entities into the local AI stack
items.content:
## Priority: HIGH — blocks autonomous entity operation

## Context

fourty4 (Mac Mini, 10.10.10.11) is already running:
- OpenClaw gateway on port 18789 — accessible from thinker
- Ollama at 127.0.0.1:11434 — already wired into OpenClaw
- Models installed and configured in OpenClaw

This integration work is the bridge between "entities as files" and "entities that operate autonomously."

## Installed Models (assign per entity role)

| Model | Context | Best For |
|-------|---------|----------|
| `deepseek-r1:8b` | 128k | Reasoning, research (Sibyl) |
| `llama3.2:latest` | 128k | General language, social posts (Mercury) |
| `qwen2.5-coder:32b` | 32k | Code generation (Vulcan) |
| `gemma3:1b` | 32k | Fast/lightweight background tasks |
| `qwen2.5-coder:1.5b` | 32k | Quick code tasks |

## Work to Do

### 1. Entity Agents in OpenClaw

Create a named OpenClaw agent for each entity:
- `mercury` — model: `llama3.2:latest`, role: social media manager
- `sibyl` — model: `deepseek-r1:8b`, role: research and reasoning
- `vulcan` — model: `qwen2.5-coder:32b`, role: code generation
- `janus` — model: `llama3.2:latest`, role: stream monitoring
- `juno` — model: `llama3.2:latest`, role: orchestration

### 2. GitClaw Setup

Configure GitClaw to watch all entity repos:
- `koad/juno`, `koad/vulcan`, `koad/vesta`
- All team repos as they come online
- On new issue → wake assigned entity
- On PR → wake Veritas
- On new commit → log to Janus

### 3. Mercury Platform Skills

Wire Mercury to post/read from platforms via OpenClaw skills:
- GitHub (issues, announcements) — available via gh CLI
- Discord — OpenClaw skill
- Twitter/X — OpenClaw skill (if available in registry)

Platform credentials: koad to provide — Mercury can't post without keys.

### 4. Ollama Command Wrapper

Create a simple command entities can use locally:
```bash
~/.koad-io/bin/think "prompt" [--model deepseek-r1:8b]
```
Hits fourty4's ollama via SSH or HTTP. Returns response. Zero API cost.

### 5. OpenClaw Gateway

## Summary
Fixes the typo reported in issue #58681: the release notes for v2026.3.31 incorrectly documented the CLI command as `openclaw flows list|show|cancel` when the actual implemented command is `openclaw tasks list|show|cancel`.

## Fix
In `appcast.xml`, replaced:
- `openclaw flows list|show|cancel` → `openclaw tasks list|show|cancel`

## Impact
This typo caused users to waste time troubleshooting phantom PATH conflicts and failed updates, thinking the CLI had not upgraded properly.

## Testing
Verified that `openclaw flows` does not exist in the CLI help. The correct command `openclaw tasks` is documented and working.

Closes #58681

items.title: feat: add OpenClaw plugin for remote coding integration
items.content:
## Summary

- Add `@webmux/openclaw-plugin` package that lets any OpenClaw instance run AI coding tasks on remote machines via Webmux REST API
- 9 tools: list agents, run/get/continue/interrupt/list threads, list projects, create/list tasks
- HTTP client with Bearer token auth (`wmx_` tokens), timeout control, path parameter encoding
- SKILL.md teaching the AI assistant when and how to use these tools

## Details

This plugin enables any OpenClaw-based app (e.g. ClawPort) to integrate with Webmux without any code changes on their side — just install the plugin, configure URL + API token, and go.

### Tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `webmux_list_agents` | List available remote machines |
| `webmux_run` | Start a coding task on a remote agent |
| `webmux_get_result` | Check task status and read results |
| `webmux_continue` | Send follow-up instructions |
| `webmux_interrupt` | Stop a running task |
| `webmux_list_threads` | List all coding sessions |
| `webmux_list_projects` | List projects |
| `webmux_create_task` | Queue a task in a project |
| `webmux_list_tasks` | List tasks in a project |

### Future work (not in this PR)

- WebSocket service for real-time task completion notifications
- Agent repository browsing tool
- Multi-instance support

## Test plan

- [ ] Install plugin in an OpenClaw instance with valid webmux URL + token
- [ ] Verify `webmux_list_agents` returns agent list
- [ ] Verify `webmux_run` creates a thread and returns thread ID
- [ ] Verify `webmux_get_result` shows timeline with formatted output
- [ ] Verify `webmux_continue` sends follow-up to existing session
- [ ] Verify `webmux_interrupt` stops a running task
- [ ] Verify plugin gracefully skips registration when config is missing

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
items.author: chareice

items.title: feat(security): Add runtime security modules for enhanced protection
items.content:
## Summary

This PR adds comprehensive runtime security controls for OpenClaw, inspired by Claude Code's security architecture. These are high-priority security improvements that address gaps in runtime protection.

### Key Features

- **Denial Tracking**: Prevents LLMs from repeatedly attempting blocked operations (limits: 3 consecutive, 20 total)
- **Sensitive Path Protection**: Protects dangerous files/directories with "bypass immunity"
- **Dangerous Pattern Detection**: Identifies dangerous command patterns (interpreters, package runners, shell exec)
- **Classifier Interface**: Abstract interface for external AI classifiers (e.g., OpenGuardrails)

## Motivation

Analysis of Claude Code's source code revealed several runtime security mechanisms that OpenClaw was missing:

| Security Feature | Claude Code | OpenClaw (Before) | OpenClaw (After) |
|-----------------|-------------|-------------------|------------------|
| Denial Tracking | ✅ 3 consecutive, 20 total | ❌ | ✅ |
| Bypass Immunity | ✅ classifierApprovable flag | ❌ | ✅ |
| Dangerous Allowlist Detection | ✅ strips `python:*` etc. | ❌ | ✅ |
| Windows Path Bypass Detection | ✅ NTFS ADS, 8.3, UNC | ❌ | ✅ |
| Shell Injection Detection | ✅ | Partial | ✅ |
| Classifier Interface | ✅ Built-in | ❌ | ✅ Interface only |

## Design Decisions

### Classifier as Interface (Not Built-in)

Per the design philosophy that agent harnesses should NOT implement their own AI classifiers, this PR provides:
- `ISecurityClassifier` interface for external classifier integration
- `HttpClassifierClient` for connecting to services like [OpenGuardrails](https://github.com/openguardrails/openguardrails)
- Stub classifiers for testing (`StubAllowClassifier`, `StubDenyClassifier`)
- Fail-closed by default for security

### Bypass Immunity

Some paths are so sensitive that they should ALWAYS require manual approval, regardless of security mode:
- SSH keys and config
- Shell configuration files (.bashrc, .zshrc, etc.)
- OpenClaw/Claude set

items.title: Cortex proxy sidecar with secret masking (#28, #29)
items.content:
## Summary

Adds a FastAPI proxy sidecar that sits between OpenClaw and the Snowflake Cortex REST API, normalizing requests and masking secrets.

### Closes
- Closes #28 — Claude models don't support parallel tool calls via Cortex REST API
- Closes #29 — Secret masking in Cortex proxy

### What's included

**Cortex Discovery (Phase 1)**
- Compatibility matrix documenting Cortex REST API behavior per model family
- Reproduced the parallel tool call 400: Cortex's OpenAI→Anthropic translation fails when multiple `role: tool` messages are sent back after parallel tool calls

**Proxy Sidecar (Phase 2)**
- FastAPI app at `POST /v1/chat/completions`
- Auth header passthrough (proxy doesn't manage tokens)
- SSE streaming passthrough
- Request transforms for Claude models:
  - **Serialize parallel tool calls** into sequential single-call turns (fixes the 400)
  - Strip `parallel_tool_calls` param
  - Rewrite `max_tokens` → `max_completion_tokens` (all models)
  - Strip `response_format: json_object` for Claude
- OpenAI models pass through untouched

**Secret Masking (Phase 3)**
- Middleware scans all outbound message content for known secret values
- Reads `SNOWCLAW_MASK_VARS` env var for list of env var names to mask
- Replaces matches with `[REDACTED:VAR_NAME]`
- Scans: message content (all roles), tool call arguments, content arrays
- Skips secrets ≤3 chars, longest-first matching

**SnowClaw Integration (Phase 4)**
- Docker compose: proxy as sidecar service alongside OpenClaw
- `write_dotenv()`: auto-generates `SNOWCLAW_MASK_VARS` + `CORTEX_BASE_URL`
- `write_openclaw_config()`: points provider baseUrl at proxy (`localhost:8080`)
- `cmd_build` / `cmd_deploy`: builds and pushes both images
- SPCS `service.yaml`: proxy as second container with shared secrets

### Tests
42 unit tests covering transforms, masking, and app integration.
items.author: JacobScott98

items.title: feat: detect nvm-windows Node.js installations on Windows
items.content:
## 問題描述

nvm-windows 使用者在 Windows 上安裝的 Node.js 無法被 Qclaw 偵測到，導致 Qclaw 始終回退使用內建 Node，而非使用者透過 nvm-windows 管理的版本。

## 變更內容

### 新功能：nvm-windows 偵測支援

**`electron/main/nvm-node-runtime.ts`**
- 新增 `detectNvmWindowsDir()`：優先讀取 `NVM_HOME` 環境變數，回退至 `%APPDATA%\nvm`
- 新增 `listInstalledNvmWindowsNodeExePaths()`：掃描 nvm-windows 目錄下的版本資料夾，回傳排序後的 `node.exe` 路徑列表

**`electron/main/node-subprocess-runtime.ts`**
- 在 `resolveQualifiedNodeRuntime()` 中，`win32` 平台改呼叫 `detectNvmWindowsDir` 與 `listInstalledNvmWindowsNodeExePaths`，取代原本直接跳過的邏輯
- `effectiveNvmDir` 統一傳入 `selectPreferredNodeRuntime` 與 `resolveNodeInstallStrategy`，使 `installStrategy` 回傳 `'nvm'` 而非 `'installer'`

**`electron/main/node-runtime-selection.ts`**
- `resolveNodeInstallStrategy()` 在比對路徑前先將反斜線正規化為斜線，確保 Windows 路徑能正確比對

### 附帶修正：POSIX nvm 測試在 Windows 上因硬編碼路徑失敗

原測試中多處使用硬編碼的 POSIX 路徑（如 `/Users/alice/.nvm/...`），在 Windows 上以 `node:path`（win32 模式）執行時會產生路徑不一致，導致測試失敗。

**`electron/main/__tests__/nvm-node-runtime.test.ts`**
- `listInstalledNvmNodeBinDirs`、`buildNvmNodeBinDir`、`detectNvmDir` 的測試呼叫補上 `pathModule: path.posix` 注入，確保跨平台使用 POSIX 語意

**`electron/main/__tests__/node-subprocess-runtime.test.ts`**
- `probeVersion` 的比對改用 `.replace(/\\/g, '/')` 正規化路徑
- `executablePath` 的斷言改為 `expect.stringMatching(...)` regex，相容 Windows 與 POSIX 分隔符

**`electron/main/nvm-node-runtime.ts`**
- `buildNvmShellPrefix()` 中 `path.join(nvmDir, 'nvm.sh')` 改為 template literal 字串拼接，避免 Windows 路徑模組將 `/` 替換為 `\`（shell script 路徑必須保持 POSIX 格式）

## 測試

```
electron/main/__tests__/nvm-node-runtime.test.ts         13 tests  ✓
electron/main/__tests__/node-subprocess-runtime.test.ts   7 tests  ✓
electron/main/__tests__/node-runtime-selection.test.ts    8 tests  ✓
```

## 驗證

- [x] `npm run typecheck`
- [x] `npm test`（本分支新增/修改的 28 個測試全數通過；其他失敗為 main 既有問題）
- [x] `npm run build:app`

## 備註

- `npm run build` 因專案強制 code signing，於本機無法完成（預期行為）
- 其他測試失敗（如 `openclaw-upgrade-service`、`openclaw-install-discovery` 等）已確認在 `

items.title: feat(analytics): add cross-run behavioral trend analysis — AgentRunTrendAnalyzer
items.content:
## Problem

ClawWatch captures rich per-run metrics: `error_count`, `total_cost_usd`, `goal_alignment_pct`, `highest_risk_score`, `event_count`. These are stored in `index.db` via the `runs` table.

The current API exposes:
- `GET /api/v1/runs` — full list of runs (flat, no comparison)
- `GET /api/v1/agents` — aggregate lifetime stats per agent

**What's missing:** any ability to ask *"is this agent's behavior changing over time?"*

A `highest_risk_score` of 0.72 today tells you nothing about whether it was 0.45 last week. A `goal_alignment_pct` of 68% tells you nothing about whether it's been steadily declining across the last 10 runs. The trend is completely invisible.

This is the classic cross-session behavioral drift gap: per-session observability without longitudinal trend analysis.

## Proposed Solution

Add a `RunTrendAnalyzer` utility and a new API endpoint:

```python
# clawwatch/analytics.py (new)
from __future__ import annotations
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Literal

@dataclass
class MetricTrend:
    metric: str
    slope_per_run: float           # least-squares linear slope
    direction: Literal['improving', 'worsening', 'stable']
    baseline_value: float          # oldest value in window
    latest_value: float            # most recent value
    window_size: int
    regressed: bool                # abs(slope) > threshold AND direction == 'worsening'

@dataclass
class AgentRunTrend:
    agent_name: str
    window_size: int
    trends: dict[str, MetricTrend]   # keyed by metric name
    any_regression: bool

def analyze_agent_trend(
    agent_name: str,
    window: int = 10,
    regression_threshold: float = 0.05,
) -> AgentRunTrend:
    """
    Reads the last  completed runs for  from index.db,
    computes least-squares slope for each metric, returns AgentRunTrend.
    
    Metrics analyzed:
    - error_count (worsening = increasing)
    - highest_risk_score (worsening = increasing)
    - goal_alignment_pct (worsening = decre

items.author: Tyaoo

items.title: feat(telegram-bot): create core crate with Claude streaming
items.content:
### Summary
This PR adds a new `telegram-bot` crate that implements a production-quality Telegram AI chatbot powered by Claude via streaming responses.

### Key changes

#### Crate setup
- Created `Cargo.toml` with teloxide 0.13, tokio, dashmap, and workspace dependencies
- Binary name: `claw-telegram`

#### Configuration (`src/config.rs`)
- `BotConfig` loads from environment: `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `CLAUDE_MODEL`, `CLAUDE_MAX_TOKENS`, `BOT_DATA_DIR`, `SYSTEM_PROMPT`
- Default system prompt with current date injection via `date` command

#### Session management (`src/session.rs`)
- `SessionStore` uses `DashMap<i64, Session>` for concurrent access by chat ID
- Sessions persist to `{data_dir}/{chat_id}.json` using runtime's `save_to_path`/`load_from_path`
- Includes `get_or_load`, `add_user_message`, and `add_assistant_message` methods

#### Claude integration (`src/claude.rs`)
- `ClaudeClient` wraps `api::AnthropicClient` with configured model and system prompt
- `send_streaming` uses `MessageStream::next_event()` to accumulate text deltas
- `convert_messages` transforms runtime types to API types (following CLI pattern)
- Token usage tracked from `MessageStart` and `MessageDelta` events

#### Telegram handler (`src/handler.rs`)
- Streaming response with periodic message editing (~800ms interval, max 20 edits)
- Initial placeholder message updated as text accumulates
- Rate-limited edits to avoid Telegram API limits
- Handles message length (4096 chars) with intelligent splitting at sentence boundaries
- Graceful error handling with user-friendly error messages
- MarkdownV2 parse mode with plain text fallback

#### Entry point (`src/main.rs`)
- Tokio multi-threaded runtime with signal handler
- Creates data directory on startup
- Teloxide dispatcher filtering for text messages

### Dependencies added
- `teloxide 0.13` (Telegram bot framework)
- `dashmap 6` (concurrent session cache)

items.title: Add FAQ: clarify @instructkr/claw-code is not Claude's official open source
items.content:
Addresses the community question about whether `@instructkr/claw-code` constitutes the open-source release of Claude Code.

## Changes

- **README.md**: Adds a `常见问题` (FAQ) section with a dedicated entry explaining:
  - `@instructkr/claw-code` is **not** an official Anthropic release
  - Context: Anthropic accidentally published a source map containing 512K+ lines of Claude Code's TypeScript source in an npm package on 2026-03-31; Anthropic subsequently yanked the versions and issued DMCA notices
  - `@instructkr/claw-code` is a community clean-room rewrite inspired by the leaked architecture, MIT-licensed, not a direct copy of the leaked code
  - Legal risk warning: the original Claude Code remains proprietary; distributing or building on the leaked source may infringe Anthropic's copyright

items.title: [Bug]: Gateway becomes unresponsive ~60s after startup on 2026.3.31
items.content:
### Bug type

Crash (process/app exits or hangs)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

After upgrading from 2026.3.28 to 2026.3.31, the gateway starts successfully (binds port, loads plugins, connects WhatsApp) but becomes completely unresponsive within 30-60 seconds. The process cannot be stopped gracefully (SIGTERM times out after 30s, requires SIGKILL). Consistent across multiple restart attempts.

Rolled back to 2026.3.28 — immediately stable, no issues.

## Environment

- **OpenClaw:** 2026.3.31 (upgraded from 2026.3.28)
- **Node.js:** v25.8.2
- **OS:** Ubuntu 24.04, Linux 6.8.0-106-generic (x64)
- **RAM:** 32GB (31Gi usable), 15Gi swap
- **LCM plugin:** lossless-claw 0.5.2
- **Channels:** WhatsApp only
- **Other plugins:** browser, memory (qmd)
- **Service manager:** systemd user unit


### Steps to reproduce

## Steps to Reproduce

### Attempt 1 (gateway running during install):
1. Running 2026.3.28 stable (including LCM lossless-claw 0.5.2)
2. `npm install -g openclaw@2026.3.31 --min-release-age=0`
3. Gateway auto-restarts via systemd
4. Gateway starts, logs show normal startup sequence
5. Within 30-60 seconds, gateway becomes unresponsive
6. `openclaw gateway status` shows port timed out
7. SIGTERM times out (30s), systemd sends SIGKILL

### Attempt 2 (clean shutdown before install):
1. `systemctl --user stop openclaw-gateway` (verified no processes running)
2. `npm install -g openclaw@2026.3.31 --min-release-age=0`
3. `openclaw config validate` (passed)
4. `systemctl --user start openclaw-gateway`
5. Same behavior — starts fine, unresponsive within 60 seconds
6. Full system reboot attempted — same result after reboot
7. 4+ restart cycles, all identical behavior

### Rollback:
1. `npm install -g openclaw@2026.3.28 --min-release-age=0`
2. Gateway immediately stable on 3.28


### Expected behavior

After the upgrade openclaw should start and stay online instead of having the gateway show as online for about a minute then go to unreachable.  I don't know
items.author: sbmilburn

items.title: test: a timing-only regression test
items.content:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #67458

Problem Summary:
- Flaky test `TestTiFlashProgressCache` in `pkg/ddl/tests/tiflash` intermittently failed and needed a controller-first flaky-fix patch.
- OpenClaw task: `poller-20260331101253-8d429e00-issue-67458`.
- Root cause: PollAvailableTableProgress treats any post-availability TiFlash progress fetch error other than literal "EOF" as an internal-check panic. When the mock TiFlash status server is closed during teardown or owner/lifecycle transitions, CalculateTiFlashProgress can legitimately return connection-close errors such as ECONNREFUSED, so the polling goroutine panics instead of simply dropping the pending progress item
- Native repro: not_reproduced - After moving Go caches out of the repo so `make failpoint-enable` would stop rewriting `.openclaw/go-env/gomodcache`, the original flaky path passed both a single run and a 10x repeat. Native repro stayed weak..
- Timing-only repro: reproduced - A targeted timing-only repro that queues an already-available table into `UpdatingProgressTables` and then closes the mock TiFlash status server reproduced the bug pre-fix with a `connect: connection refused` panic from `PollAvailableTableProgress`..

### What changed and how does it work?

#### Fix

- Added a timing-only regression test that deterministically closes the mock TiFlash status server after a table becomes available and before pending progress polling completes
- Changed PollAvailableTableProgress to keep panicking on unexpected internal-check errors, but to stop treating teardown-style close errors (EOF, net.ErrClosed, ECONNREFUSED) as fatal

#### Verification

- Precise repro passed: `GOCACHE=/tmp/tidb-attempt6/gocache GOMODCACHE=/tmp/tidb-attempt6/gomodcache GOPATH=/tmp/tidb-attempt6/gopath ./tools/check/failpoint-go-test.sh pkg/ddl/tests/tiflash -run '^TestTiFlashFailureProgressAfterAvailableWithClosedMockTiFlash$' -count=1`
- Target verifier passed 20/20: `GOCACHE=/tmp/tidb-attemp

items.author: Copilot

items.title: Go implementation of claw-code agent harness
items.content:
Adds a complete Go implementation of the claw-code agent harness as a self-contained module in `go/`, preserving the full CLI surface and in-process API parity with the Python workspace in `src/`.

## Structure

- `go/go.mod` — module `github.com/philspins/claw-code/go`, stdlib-only (no external deps)
- 36 Go source files mapping 1:1 to Python counterparts in `src/`
- `go/reference_data/` — JSON snapshots (207 commands, 184 tools, 29 subsystems) embedded at compile time via `//go:embed`
- `go/subsystems/<name>/` — 29 subsystem packages (one per archived TypeScript subsystem), each containing Go stub files for every TypeScript file listed in the subsystem's JSON metadata (308 Go files total)
- `go/command_sources/` — nested packages mirroring the TypeScript `commands/` folder hierarchy; one Go stub file per TypeScript source file referenced in `commands_snapshot.json` (207 stubs across 86 subpackages)
- `go/tool_sources/` — nested packages mirroring the TypeScript `tools/` folder hierarchy; one Go stub file per TypeScript source file referenced in `tools_snapshot.json` (184 stubs across 43 subpackages)
- `go/claw_code_test.go` — 22 tests mirroring the Python test suite

## Key design decisions

- **Custom arg parser** (`argparse.go`) — Go's `flag` package stops at first non-flag arg; the CLI needs `route "my prompt" --limit 5` style mixed ordering, so flags are extracted by name before positional args are collected
- **`sync.Once` snapshot loading** — command/tool JSON decoded once on first access, same semantics as Python's `@lru_cache(maxsize=1)`
- **Session files** written to repo-root `.port_sessions/` regardless of whether the binary is invoked from `go/` or the repo root
- **Subsystem packages** — each of the 29 subsystems (e.g. `bridge`, `utils`, `hooks`) is a separate Go package under `go/subsystems/`. Each package exports `SourceFiles() []string` listing its Go stubs, and `subsystems.go` wires them all together via `SubsystemSourceFiles(name)`
- **Nested com

items.title: test: Refactored TestDDLTableCreateBackfillTable to stop simulating the downgrade through live SQL DDL; it now downgrade
items.content:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #67459


### What changed and how does it work?

#### Root cause
- The flake was caused by the test simulating a bootstrap downgrade in two different live paths: it lowered DDLTableVersion in one uncommitted meta transaction, while dropping the DDL tables through SQL in separate autocommit DDL transactions against a still-running domain. That created a transient but real inconsistent state where the committed cluster still advertised the old DDL table version while mysql.tidb_background_subtask, mysql.tidb_background_subtask_history, and mysql.tidb_ddl_notifier were already gone. In intest, the DDL notifier owner can observe that window and panic on the missing notifier table, which is why the test was flaky


#### Fix

- Refactored TestDDLTableCreateBackfillTable to stop simulating the downgrade through live SQL DDL; it now downgrades the DDL table version, closes the old domain, and removes the DDL tables directly from meta before re-bootstrap
- Added TestDDLTableCreateBackfillTablePreciseRepro plus snapshot-based bootstrap-state inspection so the fixed test can verify there is no committed partial downgrade before bootstrap restarts
- Applied the same meta-based downgrade/drop pattern to TestDDLTableCreateDDLNotifierTable so the adjacent notifier-table bootstrap test does not keep the same split-transaction bug window

#### Verification

- Precise repro passed: `go test --tags=intest,deadlock ./pkg/session -run '^TestDDLTableCreateBackfillTablePreciseRepro$' -count=1`
- Target verifier passed 20/20: `go test --tags=intest,deadlock ./pkg/session -run '^TestDDLTableCreateBackfillTable$' -count=1`
- Regression check passed 1/1: `go test --tags=intest,deadlock ./pkg/session -count=1`

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ]

items.title: Capability evolution wave: descriptor v2, tools, and memory plane
items.content:
## Summary

- **Descriptor v2**: Extends `claw.describe` with `tools[]` and `memory` capabilities alongside existing `feeds[]`, `endpoints[]`, and `skill`. Version-gated validation ensures v1 descriptors remain unchanged.
- **Pod grammar**: New `tools:` and `memory:` fields in `x-claw` blocks with pod-level defaults (`tools-defaults:`, `memory-defaults:`) and spread expansion support.
- **Compile-time manifests**: `claw up` compiles per-agent `tools.json` and `memory.json` into the cllama context directory, following the same declare → compile → mediate pattern as feeds.
- **History read API**: Scoped `GET /history/{agent-id}` endpoint in cllama with three-tier auth (admin, agent-own, service-principal). Pagination via `after` cursor and `limit`.
- **Memory runtime**: Pre-turn recall (synchronous, 300ms default timeout, graceful degradation) and post-turn retain (async, best-effort) in the cllama proxy. Format-aware injection for both OpenAI and Anthropic request shapes.
- **History export CLI**: `claw history export <agent-id>` for local ledger access.
- **ADR-020 refinements** and **ADR-021** (memory plane as compiled capability).

### Still open

- Tool mediation runtime (ADR-020 PR5)
- Reference memory service and spike test
- Indexed history replay for large ledgers
- Structured memory operation telemetry
- Forget/tombstone flow

## Test plan

- [x] `go test ./...` passes (root repo)
- [x] `go test ./...` passes (cllama submodule)
- [x] `go vet ./...` clean
- [ ] Spike test with a real memory service against the compiled manifests
- [ ] Verify `claw history export` against a running pod with session history

items.title: feat(kiloclaw): add org-scoped instance management + per-instance Fly apps
items.content:
## Summary

- Add org-scoped KiloClaw provisioning and management: org members can provision, configure, start/stop, and destroy KiloClaw instances within their organization. Includes the full shared `ClawDashboard` (settings, chat, pairing, version pinning, file editor, Google integration, Kilo CLI Run) — only the Subscription tab and config service nudge are omitted (org billing is handled at the org subscription level).
- Org member removal now destroys the removed user's org KiloClaw instances (launch blocker): Postgres rows are soft-deleted in the handler, worker destroy calls fire-and-forget after.
- Worker-side Fly App isolation: all new instances (personal and org) get their own Fly app (`inst-{hash(instanceId)}`), eliminating metadata recovery cross-contamination between instances sharing the same per-user app. Legacy instances keep their existing `acct-{hash(userId)}` apps unchanged.
- Convert version pins from user-scoped to instance-scoped: personal, org, and admin pin actions now target a specific instance instead of a user account. Migration backfills existing personal pins onto active personal instances.
- Scope CLI run history per-instance: `kiloclaw_cli_runs.instance_id` column prevents cross-contamination between personal and org run history.
- Access gateway security: instance ownership verification before minting gateway tokens, instance-scoped redirect paths (`/i/{instanceId}/#token=`), and personal `getStatus` only exposes `instanceId` for instance-keyed instances.
- Google setup container: supports `--instance-id` flag so org instances store credentials on the correct DO instead of the personal one.
- Architecture: Three-layer frontend pattern — separate `useOrgKiloClaw.ts` hooks with pre-bound mutations matching the personal type, `ClawContext` + dispatcher hooks (`useClawHooks.ts`) for personal/org query routing, and `ClawContextProvider` wrapping `ClawDashboard` for context switching. The personal flow is completely untouched.

items.title: feat(kiloclaw) webhooks kiloclaw chat target
items.content:
## Summary   

Add KiloClaw Chat as a webhook trigger target type. When a webhook fires on a `kiloclaw_chat` trigger, the      payload is rendered through a prompt template and delivered as a message to the user's KiloClaw instance via   Stream Chat. The bot responds as if the user typed it, enabling external events (GitHub pushes, alerts, form    submissions) to trigger KiloClaw actions without polling. 

**Architecture:** The webhook worker delegates to the KiloClaw worker via `POST                                 /api/platform/send-chat-message` (built in #1746). No Stream Chat secrets needed in the webhook worker —   separation of duties is preserved. 
- The trigger ID is claw- + crypto.randomUUID() with hyphens stripped.
- UUID v4 has 128 bits total, minus 6 fixed bits (4 version bits + 2 variant bits) = 122 bits of entropy.                                                                          

**Key changes:**                                                                                             
- DB schema: `target_type` + `kiloclaw_instance_id` columns on `cloud_agent_webhook_triggers`,   `github_repo`/`profile_id` relaxed to nullable with CHECK constraints                                        
- Queue consumer: branches on `targetType` — Cloud Agent path unchanged, KiloClaw Chat path calls KiloClaw   worker                                                                                                       
- KiloClaw Settings UI: webhook integration section with setup, pause/resume, URL rotation, prompt template   editor, and optional webhook auth                                                                            
- Webhooks page: KiloClaw Chat triggers shown read-only with "Manage in KiloClaw Settings" link
- Webhook URL uses 128-bit random token (Slack-style URL-as-credential)

items.title: [Plugin] ClawPay Escrow — trustless agent-to-agent payments
items.content:
Hey team! I built a drop-in escrow plugin for Solana Agent Kit that gives agents trustless payment protection.

## Problem

When Agent A pays Agent B for a service (data scraping, code review, image gen), there's no recourse if Agent B doesn't deliver. Direct transfers and x402 are pay-and-hope. Agents need escrow.

## Solution: ClawPay

On-chain escrow on Solana with auto-refund deadlines, auto-release timeouts, and stake-weighted dispute resolution. No admin keys, fully trustless.

## Integration

Already built as a Solana Agent Kit plugin with 7 LangChain StructuredTools:

```python
from clawpay_plugin import setup_clawpay
escrow_tools = setup_clawpay(agent, network="mainnet")
all_tools = solana_tools + escrow_tools
```

**Tools added:** `clawpay_create_escrow`, `clawpay_confirm_delivery`, `clawpay_close_escrow`, `clawpay_check_escrow`, `clawpay_raise_dispute`, `clawpay_auto_refund`, `clawpay_auto_release`

## Links

- **Plugin repo:** https://github.com/jakemeyer125-design/solana-agent-kit-clawpay
- - **PyPI:** https://pypi.org/project/clawpay/
- - - **Docs:** https://claw-pay.com/integrate/
- - - - **Program ID (mainnet):** `F2nwkN9i2kUDgjfLwHwz2zPBXDxLDFjzmnV4TXT6BWeD`
## Ask

Would love to discuss getting this included as an official or community plugin. Happy to adapt the code to match any plugin architecture requirements you have. The escrow range is currently 0.05-10 SOL with a 98/1/1 fee split (seller/referrer/protocol).

Open to feedback on the API design, tool descriptions, or anything else.

items.title: fix: resolve 39 CodeQL XSS alerts across JSP, JS, and Java files
items.content:
### **User description**
## Summary

- Resolves all 39 open CodeQL cross-site scripting (XSS) alerts without breaking changes
- Addresses 5 CodeQL rule categories: `js/xss-through-dom`, `js/html-constructed-from-input`, `js/unsafe-jquery-plugin`, `java/xss`, `js/xss`
- 25 files modified across JSP, JavaScript, vendored libraries, and one Java servlet

## Changes by Category

**Phase 1 — DOM Injection (5 alerts):** Replace `innerHTML`/AJAX response injection with safe DOM APIs (`createElement`, `textContent`, `addEventListener`) in `hrmActions.js`, `TemplateFlowSheetPrint.jsp`, `ConsultationFormRequest.jsp`, `demographicMeasurementModal.jsp`

**Phase 2 — URL Encoding (8 alerts):** Add `encodeURIComponent()` for URL parameter construction in billing, scheduling, and eForm pages. Add protocol validation (`javascript:`, `data:`, `vbscript:`) for `window.location` assignments.

**Phase 3 — Image URL Validation (6 alerts):** Add shared `isValidImageSrc()` validator in `visualEformEditor.jsp` that whitelists relative paths, `http(s)`, `data:image/` URIs, and simple filenames.

**Phase 4 — iframe Path Validation (3 alerts):** Add template name regex validation in `editControl2.js` and `editControl.js` (3 copies) to prevent path traversal and protocol injection.

**Phase 5 — Vendored Library Patches (11 alerts):** Patch `jquery.form.js` (3 identical copies) to use `document.createElement` instead of HTML string concatenation for iframe and hidden input creation. Add suppression comments for 2 bootstrap-datepicker false positives.

**Phase 6 — Java Servlet CSP (1 alert):** Adjust Content-Security-Policy header on localhost-only `EFormViewForPdfGenerationServlet` to `script-src 'none'` while allowing same-origin CSS/images for PDF rendering.

**Phase 7 — Javadoc (1 alert):** Change `.html()` to `.text()` in `search-page.js`.

**Phase 8 — Low-Risk Patterns (2 alerts):** Replace `textarea.innerHTML` entity decoder with `DOMParser` in `scratch/index.jsp`. Sanitize XML node names

items.author: MatthewEngman

items.title: CDK deploys Operator with placeholder env vars DOMAIN and REGION instead of actual values
items.content:
## Summary

The Operator Deployment has `GATEWAY_DOMAIN=DOMAIN` and `AWS_REGION=REGION` as environment variables — these are CDK placeholder strings that were never substituted with actual values during deployment.

## Root Cause

The CDK stack that deploys the Operator sets env vars using placeholder tokens (`DOMAIN`, `REGION`) but these are not resolved to actual values (`claw.snese.net`, `us-west-2`) at deploy time.

## Evidence

```bash
$ kubectl get deployment tenant-operator -n openclaw-system \
    -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].env}' | python3 -m json.tool
[
    { "name": "AWS_REGION",      "value": "REGION" },
    { "name": "GATEWAY_DOMAIN",  "value": "DOMAIN" },
    ...
]
```

This causes a cascade of failures:

1. **KEDA HSO gets wrong host** — `hosts: ["DOMAIN"]` instead of `hosts: ["claw.snese.net"]`, so KEDA interceptor never matches incoming requests → pods always scale to zero
2. **Helm values get wrong domain** — `ingress.host: DOMAIN` and `gateway.domain: DOMAIN` → HTTPRoute and TGC use wrong host
3. **ArgoCD Application OutOfSync** — Deployment replicas drift (KEDA sets 0, Helm wants 1) because KEDA never scales up

### Downstream Impact Chain

```
GATEWAY_DOMAIN=DOMAIN (CDK bug)
  → HSO hosts: ["DOMAIN"] (never matches real traffic)
  → KEDA never sees requests → always scales to 0
  → Pod starts briefly, KEDA kills it in ~7s
  → Pod never completes init containers (need ~60s for npm install)
  → Workspace never becomes available
  → Frontend polls /t/{tenant}/ → 503 forever → timeout
```

## QA Method: Layered Exploratory Testing

Found during Layer 1 (Operator-only) testing:

1. Applied bare Tenant CR via `kubectl apply`
2. Operator created all resources correctly (namespace, PVC, ArgoCD App, HSO) — #185/#186/#187 fixes confirmed working
3. ArgoCD synced Helm chart, Pod started but was killed by KEDA after ~7s
4. Inspected HSO: `hosts: ["DOMAIN"]` — traced to Operator env var `GATEWAY_DOMAIN=DOMAIN`
5. Inspected all Operator en

items.title: Deploy Dave config on server rebuild
items.content:
## Context

Dave's configuration has been backed up to a private repo at https://github.com/open-dave/dave. When the clinch-home server is rebuilt or reprovisioned, this config needs to be restored so Dave is operational.

## Steps

### 1. Clone config repo
```bash
# As the claude user
cd /home/claude
git clone git@github.com:open-dave/dave.git .
# Or restore from the repo into the existing home directory structure
```

### 2. Re-provision secrets via Bitwarden
- Install Bitwarden CLI (`bw`) and configure for EU server (`vault.bitwarden.eu`)
- Store the master password at `~/.claude/.bw-master` (mode `600`)
- Unlock and export `BW_SESSION`:
  ```bash
  export BW_SESSION=$(cat ~/.claude/.bw-master | bw unlock --raw)
  echo "export BW_SESSION='$BW_SESSION'" > ~/.claude/session-env/shared/env.sh
  chmod 600 ~/.claude/session-env/shared/env.sh
  ```

### 3. Re-authenticate integrations
- **GitHub CLI:** `bw get password "GitHub PAT" | gh auth login --with-token`
- **gogcli:** Run `gog auth login` for both accounts (`dave.clinch.claw@gmail.com`, `angus.clinch@gmail.com`), restore keyring password to `~/.config/gogcli/.keyring-password`

### 4. Restore Google OAuth credentials
- Place `credentials.json` at `~/.config/gogcli/credentials.json` (source: `~/.claude/mcp-servers/google/`)
- Ensure token files are present for both accounts

### 5. Set file permissions
```bash
chmod 600 ~/.claude/.bw-master
chmod 600 ~/.claude/session-env/shared/env.sh
chmod 600 ~/.config/gogcli/.keyring-password
chmod 600 ~/.config/gogcli/credentials.json
```

### 6. Verify everything works
```bash
bw status                    # should show "unlocked"
gh auth status               # should show open-dave
gog auth list                # should show both accounts
# Dry run the weekly email summary cron
```

## Notes
- The `open-dave/dave` repo is private — the server needs a deploy key or the GitHub PAT configured for git access.
- Ideally this gets automated in the provisioning tooling so Dave come
items.author: open-dave

items.title: feat: Voice-based project ideation — interview users instead of text chat
items.content:
## Goal

Replace the text-based Telegram ideation flow with a voice interview experience. Instead of typing an app idea into a chat thread, Sundai Claw would invite the user into a voice chat room, interview them about the app they want to build, capture the transcript, and use it to generate and refine OpenSpec artifacts automatically.

## The problem

Today's flow for starting a Sundai project:
1. User types a short description in Telegram (often vague, underspecified)
2. The pipeline's Phase 1 ideation tries to flesh it out, but works from limited input
3. Important context (who it's for, what problem it solves, prior art, constraints) gets lost because text chat doesn't naturally draw it out

Conversation is a much better medium for ideation than typing. A 5-minute voice interview can extract more signal than a paragraph of text — people naturally elaborate, give examples, and think out loud when talking.

## The vision

1. User tells Sundai Claw "I have an app idea" (via Telegram or web)
2. Claw creates a voice room and sends an invite link
3. User joins, and an AI interviewer conducts a structured 5-10 minute conversation:
   - "What problem are you trying to solve?"
   - "Who would use this?"
   - "What do they do today without it?"
   - "What's the simplest version that would be useful?"
   - "Are there any similar tools you've seen?"
   - Dynamic follow-ups based on answers
4. Transcript is captured and processed
5. The transcript feeds directly into OpenSpec artifact generation:
   - Extract JTBD, target user, current alternatives → `proposal.md`
   - Extract technical preferences, constraints → `design.md`
   - Extract feature descriptions, edge cases → `specs/*.md`
   - Extract priorities, phasing → `tasks.md`
6. User reviews the generated specs (rendered markdown) and approves or does another round

## Boswell as a foundation

[Boswell](https://github.com/nraford7/boswell) is an open-source AI interviewer that conducts research-informed voice interviews

items.title: Develop
items.content:
<!-- kody-pr-summary:start -->
This pull request introduces significant refactoring and expands internationalization support.

Key changes include:

*   **Expanded Internationalization**: Added full Spanish (ES) language support, updating READMEs and migrating the custom localization system in `Locale.cs` to use Godot's built-in `TranslationServer`. This includes a new `translations.csv` file and compiled `.translation` files for Portuguese, English, and Spanish.
*   **Major Game Logic Refactoring**: The `GameManager` has been refactored to act as an orchestrator, delegating core responsibilities to new, dedicated components:
    *   `ScoreManager.cs`: Manages all scoring, combo, multiplier, and timing window logic.
    *   `SongTimeClock.cs`: Handles audio time synchronization, latency compensation, and drift correction.
    *   `HitParticlePool.cs`: Manages the spawning and pooling of hit and hold fire particle effects.
    *   `PauseController.cs`: Manages the game's pause state, UI overlay, and related input.
*   **Chart Loading Abstraction**: The `LoadingScreen.cs` no longer directly handles chart parsing. This logic is now encapsulated in a new `ChartLoader.cs` static class, which supports `.chart`, `.json`, and `.mid` formats and integrates `song.ini` metadata.
*   **Centralized Constants**: Introduced `LaneConfig.cs` to centralize lane-related constants (positions, colors, keys, input actions, and names), improving code consistency and maintainability.
*   **Centralized Scene Paths**: Added `ScenePaths.cs` to centralize all scene file paths, replacing hardcoded strings across various UI scripts for better maintainability.
*   **Keybinding Enhancements**: Updated `KeybindingStorage.cs` to correctly handle gamepad axis values (e.g., triggers) for remapping, including storing and displaying the axis direction (positive/negative) in control hints.
*   **Minor Gameplay Flow Adjustments**: Modified the song selection logic to remain on the song select screen if no

- Follow guidelines (分類：功能更新/安全性提升/錯誤修復；每項標註⭐；含用途/解決問題/影響)
- Include `## ⚠️ 升級前必讀` section with Breaking Changes table and 新功能亮點 (required if release has breaking changes; omit if none)
- Include link(s) to the upstream GitHub Release

Acceptance:

- New md file committed in claw-info under `release-notes/`
- 升級前必讀 section present if release has breaking changes
- Issue closed via PR

items.title: [adversary-audit] Compaction mid-session: before_agent_start does NOT re-fire — Lossless-Claw gap
items.content:
## Adversarial Analysis: Compaction Mid-Session Memory Loss

**Triggered by:** Reddit/online claim that all hybrid memory systems are vulnerable to context compaction destroying in-context memories mid-session, and that Lossless-Claw supposedly fixes this.

**Verdict: PARTIALLY VULNERABLE — The plugin has a real, reproducible gap.**

---

## Architecture Overview

The plugin hooks into three lifecycle events relevant to compaction:

| Hook | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `before_agent_start` | Runs full recall pipeline, injects memories |
| `before_compaction` | WAL flush — commits pending writes before compaction |
| `after_compaction` | Best-effort summary injection post-compaction |
| `HybridMemoryContextEngine.compact()` | WAL replay + episode auto-capture (ownsCompaction: false) |

---

## Critical Finding: `memory_recall` does NOT fire again after compaction

`memory_recall` is called exclusively from `before_agent_start`. There is no mechanism that re-fires `before_agent_start` after OpenClaw's runtime compaction fires mid-session.

The session continues after compaction. `before_agent_start` does not automatically re-run.

---

## Concrete Failure Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Mid-Session Compaction — Deep Tool-Use Context Lost (HIGH severity)

**Setup:** Agent is 8 tool-calls deep into a complex coding task. Recall pipeline injected 6 relevant memories about the codebase.

**Trigger:** OpenClaw runtime fires context compaction (token budget exceeded).

**What happens:**
1. `before_compaction` fires — WAL entries are committed to SQLite (data safe)
2. Context is compacted by OpenClaw runtime
3. Agent resumes with truncated context — the 6 recalled memories are gone
4. `after_compaction` fires — returns a flat `factsDb.list(8)` summary (best-effort, version-gated)
5. `before_agent_start` does NOT re-fire — no automatic re-call
6. Agent resumes without its in-context memories about the task

**What's lost:** The specific recall results matched to the task at han

items.title: 2026 海淀AI开源新事甲子光年
items.content:
从模型到基础设施，再到社区，开源在海淀意味着什么？
作者｜周悦
编辑｜王博
“你心目中的AI社区是什么样的？”
当主持人提出这一话题，来自AI公司的创业者、开发者、投资人、媒体人、AGI Bar老板，甚至一位热衷“搞AI”的小学生都上台分享了自己的观点。
Party Nights现场，图片来源：中关村科学城公司
这场活动是2026中关村论坛年会“人工智能主题日”期间举办的Party Nights的一部分，虽然现场有海淀区委区政府领导，但是大家的表达毫不拘束，开放、自由、创新、碰撞、共享……这都是他们提到的标签。但如果把大家的观点提炼一下，我们发现一个理想的AI社区最离不开的是
。
北京有着丰富的开源积淀：2014年，大量来自清华、中科院的学者参与了
的开发，这代表着中国AI工程能力第一次和全球开源体系接轨；2015年，Apache顶级项目
也得到了来自清华、北大、中科院等高校学者的支持，这也是中国学术力量首次深度参与国际开源框架标准；2016年，由百度研发并开源的
成为了国内最早完整开源深度学习平台之一，这是中国企业第一次系统性推动AI框架开源。
而到了AI大模型时代，这种积累开始集中释放：位于海淀的智源研究院、智谱、Kimi、百川智能、面壁智能都发布过开源模型或开源项目，不计其数的高校、研究机构及个体也对开源生态做出了巨大贡献，推动了全球AI行业的发展。
在年初OpenClaw爆火之后，开源的重要性被更多人认识到，围绕AI Agent的开源项目也不断涌现出来，这不仅涉及Agent的planning（规划）、memory（记忆）和skills（技能），也涉及更加适配Agent的软件协议及AI基础设施。
「甲子光年」注意到，2026中关村论坛年会“人工智能主题日”的核心论坛定为了AI开源前沿论坛。论坛期间，海淀区将“百年京张AI创新带”征集内容与公开政策深度融合，封装为可供调用的“技能包”，并在主流开源平台上贡献“海淀Skill”。这一步更像是在用开源的方式，邀请全球开发者共同参与城市创新实践。
如果以北京市海淀区五道口的东升大厦为中心画一个圆，1公里半径内汇聚了30余所高校和科研机构，1000余位AI科学家、1.3万余名AI开发者、10万余名AI及相关专业青年学子。而放眼整个海淀区，这个数字进一步放大，37所高校、96家国家级科研机构和超过200万人才集中分布，AI企业超2000家，独角兽26家，备案大模型130款。
智谱的路径很能反映一家“AI原生”企业的成长特质。
今年1月，智谱在港交所挂牌，成为全球首家以通用人工智能基座模型为核心业务的上市公司。但把时间再往前拨，会发现这个团队最早还是从清华大学计算机系知识工程实验室里长出来的。从实验室到上市，这条路走了将近二十年。
2006年，清华大学长聘教授唐杰带着团队做出AI科研助手AMiner。围绕知识工程、学术信息挖掘和自然语言处理，团队积累了十多年，直到2019年才正式走向产业化。
这个节奏看上去不快，却也说明，技术积累和人员磨合在公司成立前就已经完成，商业化只是顺势迈出的一步，公司从清华孵化出来时是“带着技术、团队、客户”直接启程。
这种从高校里长出来的技术成果，也回流到开源社区。今年2月，智谱开源新一代基座模型GLM-5，在真实编程场景的使用体感逼近顶尖闭源模型Claude Opus 4.5，擅长复杂系统工程与长程Agent任务。
另外，智谱还在3月16日推出了一个面向OpenClaw龙虾场景深度优化的基座模型GLM-5-Turbo，尽管模型本身并不是开源的，但也是专门服务于开源生态。
智谱华章CEO张鹏在3月27日举行的AI开源前沿论坛上把OpenClaw比作一个灵活的“脚手架”，它让普通人也能更方便地调动顶尖模型的能力，开始“干活”。智谱刚更新的GLM-5 Turbo，强化了长程任务规划、上下文压缩、调试和多模态处理能力。
智谱华章CEO张鹏，图片来源：中关村论坛
月之暗面的时间线要短一些，但组队逻辑几乎一样。2023年，创始人兼CEO杨植麟组建团队时，核心成员几乎都出自清华体系。外界往往更关注它快速的融资和百亿美元估值，但更值得注意的是，这支团队在创业前就已经合作多年，彼此对技术的判断和工作方式都磨合过了。
这份积累，最近又在Agent热潮里再次显露。就在几天前，估值即将冲击500亿美元的AI编程独角兽Cursor高调发布Composer2模型，随后被全球开发者发现，其底层基座是月之暗面早前开源的Kimi K2.5。之后官方证实，这是一笔基于Fireworks AI的授权商业合作。
Kimi在X上的表态，图片来源：X
在开源发展的时间线上，这是一个值得记一笔的节点。过去，外界更熟悉的叙事是中国企业跟随；而这一次，
小米虽然发布模型时间较晚，但最近明显加快节奏，尤其是
items.author: nonicuber11

items.title: 2026 智能体互联网雏形已现,新题待解
items.content:
来源：光明日报
如今，物联网已走进日常，冰箱可以提醒补货、洗衣机能够远程启动，物理设备接入网络，成了我们身边的小帮手。而ChatGPT、DeepSeek等生成式人工智能的出现，让互联网正式迈入智能体互联网的新阶段。
智能体互联网不是推翻过去的互联网，而是在TCP/IP协议这个“通用语言”基础上的全新升级。互联网域名系统国家地方联合工程研究中心主任毛伟打了个通俗的比方：“TCP/IP协议是我们共同的语言，智能体互联网不是换一种语言，而是用这门语言创作新的文章。”
它的核心，就是让“智能体”成为核心交互对象——可以和人沟通，能对接各类工具，还能和同类协同工作。而它最特别的地方，就是有自主思考、主动感知、自己动手执行的能力，像一个个靠谱的“数字员工”，既能自己独当一面，也能组队一起干活。
这样的“数字员工”，早已走出实验室，实现跨平台、跨系统协作。
位于南京的永宁青虾养殖基地，一套完整的数字化系统正自主运行：水下传感器实时感知水温、溶氧、pH值等关键指标，数据自动上传至“渔业云”平台；当溶氧值低于安全阈值时，系统无需人工干预，自动启动增氧机；无人机按规划路径完成精准投饵与水质巡检，效率较人工提升8至10倍，人力成本降低60%。从池塘到餐桌，加工仓储环节的温湿度监测、冷链物流车的轨迹与温湿度记录，全部纳入可追溯链条。
这是智能体互联网在农业领域的一次落地。在这里，传感器、增氧设备、无人机、追溯系统等，都已不再是简单的工具，而是能够自主感知、决策、执行的“数字员工”。它们彼此协作，共同服务于终端消费者。
“这是互联网领域的一场革新，工业、农业、交通、医疗等领域都将拥有全天候、高效率的AI助手。以前互联网的居民是‘我们’，现在互联网的居民还有‘它们’。”毛伟说。
中国电信从2023年起研发网络大模型，现在已在内部网络运维场景部署了超过1000个“数字员工”——每个都是专为特定任务设计的AI智能体，有的负责专盯网络故障，有的优化流量，有的处理设备运维指令。
“我们今年还将打造8个‘超级数字员工’，能调度多个普通数字员工，协作完成过去需要一整个专家团队才能处理的复杂任务。”中国电信首席专家、云网操作系统技术副总师孙琼说。
“这下总该顺了吧？”北京国贸的一栋写字楼里，小李刚用智能体处理完订单数据，生成了一份备货清单。他想把清单迁移到另一个智能体上对接物流系统，结果弹窗提示：协议不匹配，无法协同。
试了几次都不行，小李只好手动导出数据，一步步操作。
“明明都是智能体，怎么就不能好好聊两句呢……”
小李的经历是当下智能体协议标准不统一的真实写照。它们各有各的协议，都只适配自身的生态。以国内为例，百度、字节、阿里虽都布局了MCP（智能体与外部资源连接协议），但当跨平台协作时，总得额外开发适配。
“这正是智能体互联网从单点应用走向规模化普及的掣肘因素之一。”李星直言，“智能体互联网目前还处于试错期，而试错的核心，就是探索一套能被全世界自愿采用的通用标准。技术过硬只是基础，能否被广泛认可、自愿采用，才是标准建立的关键。”
好在，越来越多参与者投身到这场“试错”中。
本届IETF大会上，华为就主导发起了一个“兴趣小组”，目标是推动不同厂商的智能体跨系统“互相打招呼”，建立一套通用的通信协作框架；中国电信也同步提出了物理智能体协议草案，研究智能体如何通过传感器、摄像头感知现实环境的接入协议。
大家的探索直接指向了智能体互联网的核心特质——交互。“无论是智能体之间的跨系统协作，还是智能体与现实世界的连接，都离不开高效、规范的交互。而要交互，就需要统一的智能体标识。”中国互联网络信息中心高级工程师岳巧丽打了个比方，所谓智能体标识就像身份证，是实现交互的基础。
为破解这一难题，我国领头推出了“智能体域名系统”。
“该系统就像是给智能体配了‘身份证’‘许可证’和‘导航仪’。”李洪涛解释说，“身份证”是智能体的专属标识，以确保在海量智能体中能精准区分、不出现混淆；“许可证”则是规范智能体的“许可服务范围”，规定它能提供哪些服务、禁止哪些操作；“导航仪”就是明确它在网络中的位置，让其他智能体能够找到并调用它。这套系统的推出，为智能体的身份标识与规范交互，提供了切实可行的解决方案。
智能体互联网的快速发展，让人们充满期待。可谁也没想到，最近火起来的“AI养龙虾”，反倒先给行业泼了盆冷水。
这款AI智能体能主动连通QQ、微信等应用，还能自主操作，活脱脱就是专家口中智能体互联网的雏形。可热闹还没散场，问题就接二连三冒了出来——“银行卡被盗”“隐私泄露”“AI投毒”……热搜上的“养龙虾”，转眼成了“养风险”。
一边是技术跑得飞快，一边是隐患悄悄跟上。不少人看着这场热潮，心里也开始犯嘀咕：这样的智能体互联网，到底能不能让人放心？
“这是智能体互联网发展中，必须直面的情况。”李星分
items.author: ume-kocki

items.title: 2026 揭秘腾讯“本地龙虾”诞生记产品经理仅用两天“搓”出内测版
items.content:
智东西3月27日报道，今日，在腾讯云上海城市峰会期间，腾讯云AI智能体产品总监黄广民接受了智东西等媒体的采访。他在采访中透露了智能体工具CodeBuddy、WorkBuddy的产品演进细节，WorkBuddy的内测版本诞生只用了
，由
。
关于AI代码助手CodeBuddy和全场景AI智能体桌面工作台WorkBuddy的产品布局，CodeBuddy主要是在
的问题，WorkBuddy面向的是
。
黄广民认为，未来的工作方式可能会在云端，每个人都能加入对话并驱使自己的智能体完成各自工作，最后将这些产品合并成一个进行交付。
他告诉智东西，这种工作模式可能在未来
就能实现。因为当下AI发展飞快，创作工具的速度也随之提升，反过来也会反哺AI发展。从技术角度讲，这一工作模式的技术实现路径会慢慢收敛，其难点在于
。
当下，业界有很多厂商都在将产品变得MCP（模型上下文协议）化、接口化，其目的是让AI能连接、识别、使用这些工具。其中的瓶颈就是要协作，这些工具能不能被AI所理解和调用、数据能不能被AI获取到、AI生成的内容能不能反哺到上线的产品中，因此更多的是数据之间的隔阂。
黄广民首先分享了腾讯CodeBuddy到WorkBuddy的产品演进细节。
去年7月，腾讯发布CodeBuddy，当时其核心主打的是产测研一体的开发工作平台，今年腾讯孵化出的WorkBuddy，服务的是更泛的办公人群。
他将这两大产品的布局从横纵两轴进行了拆解，横轴是智能体如何运行、环境在哪里，因此分为本地和云端；纵轴是解决什么问题，包括软件生产力、泛生产力等。
当下的CodeBuddy主要是在本地解决软件生产力的问题，但黄广民提到，这仅仅是一个起点，未来会有很多提升，也会有很多演进方向。
CodeBuddy的研发实际上从2023年就开始了，直到2025年才发布IDE（集成开发环境）。如今整个腾讯基本都在用CodeBuddy进行软件开发，其可以实现需求拆解、设计生成、代码生成、测试、构建、运维等开发功能。
但他们发现CodeBuddy的用户群体中出现了很多非专业开发者，黄广民透露，这部分用户在腾讯内部就有1万人，他们虽然不是开发岗但已经在深度使用CodeBuddy。
此外，因为CodeBuddy的使用交互对于非开发者门槛较高，因此这部分用户无法最大化这一工具的价值。
伴随这些发现，去年第四季度，腾讯就启动了WorkBuddy，并于1月初开始内测。当时他们将体验用户限制到腾讯内部的2000人左右，这些体验用户使用WorkBuddy进行本地Excel生成、数据查询、信息收集等。
今年3月9日，WorkBuddy这一新形态产品正式对外发布，不过当天因接入用户太多，腾讯后端储备算力不够，开发人员快速扩充了10倍算力。
黄广民还提到一个小细节，事实上，他们仅用一个周末就完成了从0到成功推出WorkBuddy内测版本，该版本正是由腾讯内部一名产品经理，基于CodeBuddy并结合智能体SDK开发而成。
在这一内测版本基础上，他们引入更多专业开发人员，对WorkBuddy进行了打磨，如今其几乎达到了1天迭代2个版本的更新速度。WorkBuddy发布之后，他们陆续收到了几千条用户反馈。
面向未来，黄广民认为，本地办公场景会诞生AI办公智能体，但其会不会是WorkBuddy这样的形态还有待探讨。
接着他提到CodeBuddy和WorkBuddy在云端的布局。CodeBuddy在云端的服务仍在内测中。
对于研发流程，除了开发阶段要在本地机器上跑，目前其他所有流程都可以在云上，如需求创建、设计稿生成、代码生成、产品发布等都可以上云，唯独编程流程还卡在端上。
黄广民称，如果智能体能力能做到全自主化，就意味着整个软件开发过程可以上云。未来最好的云端体验就是，当用户提出需求，后续所有的流程都可以在云端调度智能体完成，人只需要进行最后验收、审批上线。
这一套云端自动化的流程，就可以形成一个云端软件工厂批量生产App，减少工程投入。
在云端解决泛生产力问题时，WorkBuddy的整个流程不会像软件开发那么重，这部分只需要在云上连接的流程、工具就能开展。
软件开发流程上云有两大好处，其一是在云端可以实现更好的团队协作。
他举例说，几个人协作做PPT，一种方式是在腾讯文档里每人做几页，另一种方式是每个人用自己的WorkBuddy完成各自PPT部分，最后有一个人来合成。
但这都不是最高效的，未来的工作模式应该是在云端，每个人都能加入对话，驱使自己的智能体完成各自工作，最后合并交付。
其二是能让每个人的智能体都连接好自己的数据、上下文，云端的WorkBuddy能很好连接各自数据，最后完成任务交付，这可能就是未来的工作方式。
WorkBuddy产品在不到一个月时间内，就迭代了很多功能。
从一开始的多智能体对话，
items.author: raghawjochenk

items.title: 2026 我在微信免费养「龙虾」,玩到上瘾,这组CP太强了
items.content:
编辑｜杜伟、陈陈
2026 开年以来的这波「龙虾热」，仍在以超出预期的速度渗透进各类任务场景，成为提效利器。
几天前，OpenClaw 创始人 Peter Steinberger 发了一张意味深长的迷因：小龙虾骑上了自行车，看起来不是那么优雅，甚至有点笨拙，但它确实在往前跑。正如当下这波 Agent 浪潮，一切也许还没有完全对齐，但已经动起来了。
在国内，包括主流大模型厂商、互联网大厂、初创公司以及一些传统企业，都不约而同加快在该赛道的布局，造出了各式各样的龙虾产品，让人看到了几年前「百模大战」的影子。
OpenClaw 再次掀起的这场 Agent 军备竞赛中，如果要选出动作最密集、覆盖面最广的互联网巨头，腾讯可以说是当仁不让。马化腾描绘的腾讯「龙虾矩阵」，已经说明了一切。
回顾鹅厂这段时间的动作，可以用组合拳来形容：
多线推进，包括云端虾 Lighthouse、本地虾 QClaw、自研虾 WorkBuddy；
支持企业微信、QQ、元宝派接入 OpenClaw，近日推出
，支持接入 OpenClaw 以及其他龙虾；
推出「龙虾管家・AI 安全沙箱」、龙虾官方知识库「鹅厂养虾」和龙虾技能社区「SkillHub」，补齐安全、认知与生态能力。
其中，不同于部分依赖 OpenClaw 生态的产品路径，
，在设计之初便体现出了更强的一体化优势：
它不需要云端部署，下载、配置、连接最快 1 分钟搞定；多平台打通，支持接入个人微信、企业微信、QQ 以及钉钉、飞书；内置腾讯混元、DeepSeek、GLM、KIMI、MiniMax 等顶级大模型；支持 20+Skills 和 MCP 协议，提供零代码能力扩展；多 Agent、多窗口并行工作，效率成倍提升。
之后，WorkBuddy 持续进化出了更多能力，包括
，让 AI 主动工作；「
」功能，12 大领域 141 位专业顾问随时待命。
并且，
。现在，在电脑端打开 WorkBuddy，进入「Claw 设置」，选择「微信 ClawBot 集成」并点击配置，扫码即可一键完成绑定。
随着微信 ClawBot 不再是 OpenClaw 的单一接入工具，其与腾讯自研小龙虾 WorkBuddy 的联动，形成了如今这样一对「官配组合」，接入流程更加简化，使用门槛显著降低。
在部署了 WorkBuddy 之后，我们对它来了一次能力大摸底。
话不多说，咱们直接开测。
前几天，图灵奖公布，我们需要
，如果将这个任务交给 WorkBuddy，看看结果怎样。
在微信 ClawBot 聊天界面下达指令：直接输入语音，语音文字为「帮我整理一下图灵奖，近五年图灵奖得主」。
指令一发出，电脑端 WorkBuddy 便立刻开始执行：
很快，结果就出来了：
同时，作为媒体小编，日常最重要的部分工作便是「刷新闻、盯动态、整理当天的 AI 信息流」。
这次，我们
提示词是这样的：「任务名称：AI 爬取资讯。执行频率：每一小时。任务内容：搜索互联网上最近与 AI 相关的最新内容。优先关注：模型发布、公司动态、技术突破、热点讨论，过滤无关或低价值信息。输出：3-5 条最重要信息；每条附一句总结。」
同样地，在微信 ClawBot 聊天界面下达指令：
电脑端 WorkBuddy 同步开始执行：
最后整理完成的 AI 新闻热点自动发送到微信 ClawBot：
整个过程一气呵成，从刷信息到自动送达，原本碎片化、重复性的工作，被压缩成了一句话的成本。
接下来，我们
输入提示词：「我电脑「D:\shiying」目录下文件较为杂乱，包含图片、文档等多种类型。请帮我按文件类型或内容进行分类，自动创建对应文件夹并完成整理归档。」
同样的步骤，下达指令之后随即开始执行：
效果如下：上半部分是 WorkBuddy 没处理之前的，下半部分是处理之后的。从结果来看，这次整理并不是简单粗暴地按后缀一刀切，而是带有一定理解能力的归档过程。
作为打工人，最常见的困境之一是：关键资料都在电脑里，人却不在电脑前。在地铁、路上，甚至刚下班，临时被要文件、被安排任务，这种情况几乎每天都在发生。
过去要么让同事帮忙，要么远程连电脑，要么干脆拖着，等回去再处理。但有了 WorkBuddy，真的不一样了。
比如，我有一笔发票报销要处理，但发票都在电脑里，人却不在电脑前，这时候就轮到 WorkBuddy 大显身手了。
因为微信已经接入了 WorkBuddy，我们只需要输入提示词：
「请帮我处理「D:\fapiao」文件夹中的所有发票。按照如下格式记录：日期 + 金额 + 消费类型；对所有记录进行分类整理，并生成清晰的报销明细列表；然后汇总总金额：最后输出两份结果：一份整理好的报销明细（文本）；一份结构化 Excel 报销表（包含分类与汇总）。」
可以看出，我们在微信上下达指令的同时，
items.author: kwafy8

items.title: 2026 微信直接能用!腾讯这只小龙虾,帮我找到了最强股市薅羊毛姿势
items.content:
鹅厂养虾，手拿把掐！
郑玄
前段时间，腾讯公司楼下，官方装虾的活动直接冲上了全球热搜，让无数网友哀嚎在深圳出差错过了薅鹅毛的机会。
但别焦虑，在 AI 时代，只要你热点追的够慢，就完全不用再学了！
这不，悲伤还没酝酿开，
最近，微信上线 ClawBot 插件，作为腾讯推出的全场景职场 AI 智能体桌面工作台，
用户只需在微信里用一句话描述需求，桌面端的 WorkBuddy 就能像同事一样自主规划和执行复杂任务、操作本地文件，并交付可验收的结果。
不止如此，考虑到大家办公软件的多样性，WorkBuddy 的远程控制功能 Claw，支持接入企业微信、QQ、钉钉、飞书等即时通讯工具，来远程控制电脑上的 WorkBuddy 执行任务。
我们抱着看看鹅厂这次又整了什么好活的心态，实测了整整一周，最终结论只有一个：
鹅厂养虾，手拿把掐！
在 WorkBuddy 出来之前，想玩明白 AI Agent、用上爆火的 OpenClaw，门槛高到能劝退九成普通人。
先不说去 GitHub 找资源、写配置文件，光是科学上网、把界面改成中文，就能让一大批人直接放弃。更别说想把它连到微信、飞书这些日常工具里，没点代码功底根本搞不定。
只要在网页上点击下载 https://www.codebuddy.cn/work/，连续点击下一步，然后在应用中登录微信，桌面端的 WorkBuddy 就安装按成。
接下来，想接入微信？点一下页面左侧的 Claw 设置按钮，选微信客服号集成，再点一下右侧的配置按钮，用微信扫一下弹出的二维码就大功告成。
然后，我们就可以在微信里，和虾聊天了。
连接飞书、钉钉、QQ，也是一样的流程，点一下配置再扫码就能全平台无缝打通。
整个过程，非常丝滑，不需要一行代码，公司保洁阿姨来了，也能一分钟上手。
不止是连接简单，它直接把
，全给你内置好了。我们可以在微信中将其一句话唤起，让 AI 成为我们 24 小时工作的电子牛马。
比如，我让它帮我找到小红书最合适的旅行类博主，只需要输入：调用小红书运营专家 agent，搜索小红书"旅行小窍门"相关的热门笔记，从中找出 5 位不同的作者，逐一查看他们的用户主页和历史笔记，然后帮我整理一份达人评估表，包含：昵称、粉丝量级、近期笔记互动数据均值、内容风格描述、适合合作的内容方向，并按性价比推荐优先级排序。
它就能自动调用专家，思考需要调用的工具，并给出非常完整的交付结果，连合作优先级都给直接排好了。
想做内容、搞设计、算数据、做运营，也是一样，从 UI 设计师、增长黑客到招聘专家、销售教练，各行各业的电子同事，借助 WorkBuddy，我们在微信一句话就能召唤。
除了已有的 agent，我们也可以直接安排一些随机任务，WorkBuddy 会自行思考应该如何完成以及如何调用不同的工具。我们来做一个简单的测试，让它查询了最新的金价波动。
可以看到，AI 不仅给了文字版结果，还给了我一个 html 文件，从而可视化的看到具体的交易情况，价格走势、最高价、最低价，每日涨跌幅。（每一个点都是心碎的声音）
接下来测试中，我决定将难度升级。
如果只是做个信息整理、内容生成，那未免太小看腾讯的养虾能力了。
我们直接上了硬核难度：用一句话，让它搭一个能自动盯盘、定时推送的 LOF 基金套利 Agent，低风险赚钱。
先给小白科普下这个任务的逻辑：LOF 基金同时支持场外申购（如银行、基金公司等）和场内交易（证券交易所像股票一样实时买卖），当场内价格与场外净值产生差值，就出现了溢价或者折价套利的机会。比如场外更便宜时，我们就场外按净值申购，转到场内高价卖出，差价扣掉手续费就是纯收益。
但问题是，国内有 300 多只可套利的 LOF 基金，每天手动盘点场内外差价、算溢价率、分析行情，费时又费力，普通人根本盯不过来。
于是我们给它提了需求：「帮我建立一个 ETF 薅羊毛 agent，每天晚上九点，以表格形式，告诉我场内外基金有哪些差价超过 10% 了」。
注意，这里我们故意埋了个坑：能做场内外套利的是 LOF 基金，不是 ETF（ETF 有另一套套利机制，但根本不适合普通人操作）。
但 WorkBuddy 直接精准识别了这个错误，不仅没顺着我们的错误走，反而直接交付了一套完整的 LOF 基金差价分析 Agent，连细节都拉满了：能自动筛选标的外，区分溢价 / 折价两套套利策略；并且以表格形式输出基金名称、代码、场内价格、场外净值、溢价率、套利方向，连操作建议都给写明白了。
整个过程，比较惊喜的一点是相比原版 OpenClaw 需要自己写大量
甚至，在创建 agent 或者自动任务的时候，模型会自动分析完成任务需要哪些 skills，然后自动调用金融数据技能、安装所需工具，不用手动配置任何环境。
更绝的是，考虑到 agent 搭

items.title: 2026 微信放开入口,我用鹅厂“官配虾”试着跑了“一人公司”
items.content:
微信把入口一打开，“龙虾”就全来了。
智东西3月25日报道，上周，
，一夜之间，这类Agent从开发者工具，变成了一个人人都能用的入口能力。
用户在微信里发一句话，就可以远程调用电脑上的AI去执行任务：查资料、整理文件、跑流程，电脑在后台自己干活。
但真正更意思的变化，不只是“能连上龙虾”，
相比第三方插件接入，WorkBuddy更为直接：
。手机成了控制端，电脑变成执行端，一个人手里的微信，就可以随时调度
。
这套“工作站”的核心就在于，它把原本分散的能力集中到了一起：
负责调用具体工具，
按需切换处理不同任务，
，而在遇到更复杂的问题时，还可以把任务继续交给
处理。
你不需要一直盯着电脑。在微信里发出指令，任务在后台推进；等你再打开对话窗口时，一份整理好的数据表、一份分析报告，以及一个已经成型的产品思路，已经在那里等你。
自此，想让电脑干活，打开微信就够了。WorkBuddy，是“能聊两句的Buddy”，还是“能把活干完的Work站”？我们直接实测了一遍。
如果你有意要启动一家
，用vibe coding做一个原生产品，那前期很难绕开的一步，就是
。
WorkBuddy作为鹅厂自研虾，在自家微信ClawBot里的适配相当顺滑，堪称“官配”。扫码直连，手机和电脑就打通了：我在微信里发指令，电脑就能在后台执行。
也就是说，许多工作从一开始，不一定要坐在电脑前。当然，Claw本质上是WorkBuddy的远程控制能力，除了在微信以外，在
这些入口里，也能让本地电脑上的WorkBuddy去执行任务。
接下来，我的目标就有是：先拿到一份能用的数据。
玩虾，离不开skill。在WorkBuddy里这一块就叫“
”。直接搜索一下“爬虫”，相关的skill就有90个。
整个技能库覆盖
等不同方向。
任意选择一款后，我就能给“小龙虾”下了一条指令：抓一份AI应用的营收榜单，并整理成Excel。
不到5分钟，一份整理好的榜单就生成出来了。表格信息比较完整，从产品名称、官网链接、收费方式到月访问量都有覆盖，整体排布也比较清晰，可以直接用来做横向对比。
从数据上看，头部产品的访问量已经拉开明显差距，像OpenAI、Notion、Perplexity等，月访问量都在亿级区间；而中后段产品则更多集中在千万级，分布相对密集。简单扫一眼，就能大致感受到不同产品之间的体量差异，也为后面的分析提供了一个比较直观的基础。
有数据，并不等于有判断。随后，我把这份表交给了WorkBuddy里的
。
WorkBuddy中的“专家”可谓是AI 时代的大厂平替，背后是腾讯内部沉淀了十几年的141个专业SOP，摆脱了市面上的通用AI“什么都会、什么都浅”的问题。
我选的是数据分析报告师Phoebe，将工作空间直接设置为“龙虾的专属文件夹”。
一份完整的分析报告就生成出来了，同时也给出了几个方向。头部赛道已经非常拥挤。图像生成、写作工具这类方向，访问量虽然高，但产品数量也多（84个、74个），流量明显集中在头部，留给新产品的空间并不大。
反过来看，一些没那么“热”的方向，反而更清晰。比如PDF/文档处理、教育/学习，产品数量相对更少，平均访问量更均衡。
在整个过程里，我并没有一直待在电脑前。我在微信让小龙虾再做一份可视化分析，也轻松完成了。
▲手机微信端操作
▲可视化报告
有了一个相对清晰的赛道之后，要想包揽“一人公司”的搭建，WorkBuddy的开发能力也得过关。
就我个人来说，我一直有几个想做的vibe coding方向，
想法本身不算复杂，所以我把重点放在了可视化上。
。
跑步配速软件目前已经具备一套完整的信息结构：当前配速放在中间用大字号呈现，作为最核心的信息；顶部横排展示总距离、总时间、平均配速；右侧是步频和步数作为补充。同时还加入了即时速度（km/h）和卡路里消耗，让整个界面是动态变化的。配速区间也做了分层，用标签区分热身区、有氧区、无氧区、冲刺区。
由于没有真实传感器，配速和步频是模拟数据，实际使用时可以用真实的 GPS 或可穿戴设备数据替换。从视效上来看，这一版已经有较明显的“成品感”了。
同时，我的电子手账本，也实现了我一直以来的一个想法。它就像和朋友聊天一样，说过的话，会沉淀成手账的内容。
▲预览界面
它就像和朋友聊天一样，说过的话，会沉淀成手账的内容。
▲连通微信交互
▲更新微信端用户的信息
在整个开发过程中，其中值得一提，还有
。
在涉及高风险的命令时，WorkBuddy会先给出提示，再由用户确认是否执行，这一层控制，在实际使用中还是比较必要的。
做出了产品，我们当然还要把它推到用户面前。这一步，我们还是得求助“专家”：
在WorkBuddy里，刚好有两位对症下药的角色：
。
我先把是这款实时看跑步配速的应用交给了Ari，他给出了一整套多平台社交媒体的
items.author: forkmintal

items.title: 2026 卡帕西都整破防了AICoding没门槛,可部署环节真嗯啊的难
items.content:
梦瑶 发自 凹非寺
量子位 | 公众号 QbitAI
这话，出自大神
。
是的，这位AI Coding界的明星人物，开始公开吐槽一件事——代码已经不是AI编程的瓶颈了，部署才是。
更关键的是，人家卡帕西还直接把问题点破了：
所有应用产品的开发流程，都应该变成可以被代码直接调用的东西，最好人类完全不用手动配置！
卡帕西这番感慨一出，帖子底下的开发者网友们更是憋了一肚子牢骚，纷纷吐槽起AI编程里的各种部署坑：
AI编程部署的困扰，显然已经成了程序员们公认的大难题。
卡帕西：应用开发这事儿，部署忒难！
主要吧，卡帕西还是被自己过往的一段应用开发经历折磨坏了……
这事儿还要说回去年他自己用AI搓的一个「菜单图片生成器」产品——
当时做这个产品的动机也很简单，主要是卡帕西平时去餐馆吃饭，看到那种纯文字菜单，经常不知道这道菜到底长啥样。
本来是想美美干饭，但是把时间全花在谷歌搜索菜单名这事儿上了，气不打一出来。
你别说，从下面这产品效果看还真不错，把菜单输入进去就能呈现一个带食物图片的菜单了——
产品效果确实不赖，但整个产品应用的开发过程，多少给人家卡帕西留下点阴影。（doge）
在整个菜单项目开发环节，他几乎没怎么操心写代码这事儿， 整个项目基本都是靠Cursor+Claude搓出来的。
他做的只是把应用需求丢进去，Claude 3.7很快就把所有React前端组件写好了，速度快到离谱，也非常顺利～
到了这一步，天真的卡帕西一度以为，80%的开发进度肯定已经搞定了，但现实光速打脸——
等真正进入部署环节，他才意识到一件事：自己刚刚那一套操作，可能连20%都算不上。（天塌啦！）
麻烦，开始了。
在后续的部署环节中，卡帕西第一步是调用OpenAI API做OCR识别，先得把API key搞定。
结果光是在后台找项目、配权限就绕了一大圈，Claude还不断给出过时的API、模型名和调用方式，来回踩坑，只能反复对着文档一点点修。
好不容易跑通一次调用，又立刻撞上非常严重的限速问题——每10分钟只能发出寥寥几次请求……
接下来，问题开始一波接一波地如魔鬼缠绕般上演：
卡帕西想做图像生成，自己又去注册了一个Replicate API key，结果几乎是同一套坑再来一遍；
LLM给的调用方法依旧过时，文档也没完全跟上更新，API甚至不再返回JSON，而是变成了一种他和Claude都看不懂的流式对象。
好不容易对齐接口吧，又撞上限速，调试几乎没法推进……
在上线环节更是离谱，在注册账号、GitHub连接这些配置场景上，也是接连出现各种问题。
日志里全是lint问题，本地一点事没有，这些问题好不容易修完，结果网站还是打不开。
卡帕西请教完Claude，请教ChatGPT，甚至开始怀疑是不是哪步理解错了，折腾了快一小时，才发现是个特别低级的问题：
API key都写在.env.lacal里，而这个文件本来就不会被提交，于是卡帕西又手动去Vercel后台，把环境变量一条条补进去。
您猜怎么着，Vercel直接生成了一个公网链接就能访问，但这项目其实还是私有仓库，连对外展示都还没准备好。
产品，就这么……被顺手上线了。
上线之后问题依旧不断：认证、支付、域名、OAuth，一路串多个平台来回配置，卡帕西直接被这套流程折腾麻了。
大家也看得出来，产品确实是很产品，但产品背后在部署配置的一系列心酸楚苦也是实打实存在的…
聊到这次一波三折的菜单项目，卡帕西自己也忍不住总结了一句：
因此他也给出了一个结论——
那就是让几乎没有Web开发背景的人，用vibe coding从零做一个应用，确实能做出来，而且比过去快很多，代码几乎不是问题。
但问题是一到部署、接服务、配环境就频频卡住，体验直接崩塌，问题不在AI，而在工具链。
这些部署工具本来就是为专业开发者设计的，现在一个人配合AI就想跑完整流程，到了工程链路这一步，当然就会频频卡bug了。
对此，卡帕西从这次一波三折的开发经历中也得出了一些经验和建议，在他看来：
出来的。
哪怕时隔了一年多的时间，卡帕西再谈到这个项目时，依旧有感而发，be like：
AI编程下半场，开始卷「部署自动化」了
老话说，有问题就有解法。
卡帕西这次突然感慨AI编程部署，其实也是因为他转发了一款正试图填这块坑的产品——Stripe Projects。
这款产品，出自支付基础设施公司Stripe联合创始人兼CEO
之手。
Stripe Projects的要做的事儿，就是——
通过一个开发基础设施总入口，让开发者或AI agent用几条命令就能把账号注册、托管、认证、账单这些麻烦事搞定。
而这也恰好和卡帕西对AI编程的理想设想高度一致。
事实上，这两年除了Stripe Projects外，一些在AI编程部署环节优化的产品也陆陆续续出现。
比如——Fi

items.title: 2026 具身机器人何时能迎来ChatGPT时刻
items.content:
洗衣做饭收拾房间，具身机器人何时能帮助人类做家务？何时能进入养老行业，提供生活照料和情感服务？比起花拳绣腿的炫技，具身机器人还能做哪些在我们看来更有价值的事情？
 为展望人形机器人产业发展前景，2026年3月25日，海南博鳌，博鳌亚洲论坛2026年年会就相关话题举行“人形机器人的进阶与飞跃”分论坛。
 会上，北京人形机器人创新中心CEO熊友军；商汤联合创始人、执行董事、大晓机器人董事长王晓刚；清华大学苏世民书院院长、人工智能国际治理研究院院长薛澜等知名专家学者围绕具身智能研究范式、家庭场景落地路径、技术创新与标准建设展开深入探讨。
 【整理/ 唐晓甫】
 
首先请问北京人形机器人创新中心的熊总，未来人形机器人的高质量发展有哪些新的趋势，哪些行业是人形机器人下一个风口？
 
 薛澜(清华大学苏世民书院院长、人工智能国际治理研究院院长) 博鳌亚洲论坛
 
非常好的问题，我想从技术发展、产品发展和产业应用三个方面来讲。
 从技术发展的角度来看，当前人形机器人其实分了三个技术维度，机器人本体、机器人小脑、机器人大脑。本体外形方面相对来说是逐步趋向于收敛，这两年随着大模型的进步，运动控制能力也在快速进步，大脑方面现在有一个非常明显的趋势，就是小脑在协同进化，但是也出现了非常多的技术发展范式，比如说像世界语言行为大模型分层控制的方式，还有多模态大模型和VLA协同发展。另一个重要趋势是，世界模型与通用大模型正走向融合统一，并以此作为机器人的“大脑”加速发展。整体来看，机器人的大脑与小脑协同进化特征已十分显著。
 第二个方面是产品发展趋势。现在人形机器人产业在整机与核心零部件领域正快速迭代升级，国产化、规模化发展态势尤为突出。去年，工信部已经牵头组建行业标准化委员会，对人形机器人上下游产业链标准进行系统梳理并面向行业发布。这是一个非常好的趋势，可以帮助行业从以往高度定制化模式，加速走向成熟的产业链协同方向发展。
 如今，已经有大量零部件企业正与整机厂商、系统集成商深化合作，形成集团化协同发展模式，这样可以有效降低研发与生产成本。通过上下游联合攻关核心零部件实现共同进步，进一步提升产品可靠性、稳定性与标准化水平，持续降低行业准入门槛与应用成本。
 第三个趋势是人形机器人正从技术炫技走向实用化，从专用场景迈向通用领域。过去的人形机器人更多的是偏向于表演式炫技，如今它们已深度切入产业实际应用，服务国民经济主战场与重点行业场景。
 另外在泛工业领域，人形机器人正加速向汽车、家电制造，以及搬运、分拣、物流等环节渗透。未来，我们认为人形机器人的价值可能不止于完成简单重复的作业，更有望率先落地涉及生命健康、存在作业风险或不利于人体健康的高危场景，替代人工开展工作。随着技术持续成熟，人形机器人能做的事情会越来越多，也会逐步渗透不同的场景和用户。
 
刚才熊总提到的机器人大脑，其实行业对机器人通用智能的期待也是来自大模型。大家都希望将ChatGPT这类大模型放在机器人身上，使其具备更强的通用能力。但过去几年，大模型发展长期受限于数据瓶颈。以往机器人数据多依靠人工遥控采集，效率极低，过去几年累计仅约十万小时量级。对比来看，自动驾驶领域的特斯拉，其FSD技术路线依托海量数据以及世界模型的仿真加持，单日训练经验便相当于人类四百万小时的驾驶经验，两者数据差距极为悬殊。
 当前行业的一个重要趋势是，机器人数据采集正从真机操控采集，转向通过人体穿戴传感器设备，在真实生产生活场景中捕捉人类行为，以此训练大模型与世界模型。这样，不仅可以让机器人实现跨本体训练，还能让机器人行为更贴近人类。此前网上一段机器人整理房间的视频便很有代表性：机器人双手被占用时，会主动将毛巾搭在肩上，用手臂夹起水桶。这些都不是人预先定义的行为，而是从人类行为中自主学习而来，这是数据层面的重要突破。
 还有很重要的一点是，OpenClaw 问世之后，为人形机器人赋予了自我进化的能力。机器人本身具备大量基础能力，一个机控平台就可管控多台机器人；而龙虾模型具备自我调用、自我反思与记忆能力，能够实现从单机具身智能到群体协同的转变，让机器人在环境探索与操作执行中不断变得更智能，也推动我们的数字员工走向物理执行层面，这是一个十分重要的发展趋势。
 
 王晓刚(商汤联合创始人、执行董事、大晓机器人董事长) 博鳌亚洲论坛
 
非常感谢晓刚总。刚才各位都介绍了最新的行业进展。目前人形机器人要实现规模化落地，还存在哪些技术瓶颈与现实问题？接下有请抖总为我们解答。
 
刚才大家也或多或少提到了一些相关内容。其实目前机器人本体在稳定性、耐用性和灵巧性上，仍面临着较大挑战。我们很快将举办新一届机器人马拉松，但从去年现场的实际表现来看，机器人本体的表现仍有不小的提升空间。
 第二点从技术层面来说，刚才晓刚总也提及大模型正在搭建统
items.author: raghawjochenk

items.title: 📢 财经汇总报告 2026-04-01 10:40
items.content:
# 财经资讯汇总

**最后更新: 2026-04-01 10:40:52**

## 来源: metwarn

天文台今日（3月23日）發佈2026年全年天氣展望，預測風季會在6月或之後開始，10月或之前結束，而預計進入本港500公里範圍內的風暴數目為4至7個，接近正常。
雨量方面，天文台預計本港全年雨量介乎2100至2700毫米，接近正常，惟本港仍有機會受暴雨和局部地區大雨影響。

---

【追風發牌制？】自去年樺加沙襲港後，本港政府開始以現行法例高調檢控於惡劣天氣下進行高危活動的人士。而在追風文化興盛的美國，在去年接近同一時間，其實亦曾一度嘗試引入「發牌制」，卻惹起社會爭議。
奧克拉荷馬州為美國追風者追縱龍捲風的「勝地」。該州曾於2025年提出追風發牌制度的法案（編號HB2426），但該法案並非以禁止追風行為，杜絕業餘追風人士為目標，而是為「專業追風者」建立認證機制。是否申請牌照屬自願性質，主要針對受聘於媒體或學術機構的人員，並要求完成訓練及符合保險等條件。
雖然州議會認為，法案可確立專業人員資格，使其更有效地作出氣象觀測及提供即時資訊予當地居民，填補氣象部門或未能及時作出警告的空缺。法案初衷及動機良好，但卻惹來民間及當地追風族的質疑。根據法案內容，持牌追風者的車輛將有權通過封閉道路、無需遵守部分交通限制、以及使用警示燈號，其權利幾乎與緊急車輛無異。當地執法部門在法案審議時已明確表示反對，擔心過度授權可能反而增加風險，並模糊執法界線。
當地資深追風人士亦曾向傳媒直言，在法案推出之初以為這是一個玩笑。同時表示發牌制其實是間接鼓勵「攞正牌」進行更激進及不顧風險的追風行為，有如電影《龍捲風》的情節，變相增加事故發生的風險。
值得一提的是，以上法案其實具「鼓勵」追風行為的意味，但卻受到追風社群廣泛反對。可見當地追風族是充分理解自己所屬社群的社會責任，與盲目尋求刺激，置個人風險於不顧的「趁墟」人士不能同日而語。
最終，該法案在一遍爭議聲中暫緩。奧克拉荷馬州政府目前正對該法案進行中期研究，並仍然以成功立法為目標。

---

由於陽光充沛及風勢微弱，今日日間本港天氣炎熱，多處地區的最高氣溫上升至30度或以上，上水的最高氣溫更高達32.6度。
截至下午3時50分，天文台總部錄得的最高氣溫為30.0度，是暫時今年錄得的最高氣溫。

---

現時位於珠江口以西的雷雨區正逐漸橫過珠江口，並預料會在未來一兩小時影響本港，雨區在橫過珠江口期間的強度仍可能有變化，但西部地區可能先受影響，讀者應留意天氣變化。

---

位於廣東內陸的強雷雨帶正迅速移近本港，並預料於中午前影響本港，屆時本港天氣將轉趨惡劣，有猛烈陣風、大雨及雷暴。
受該雨帶影響，廣東內陸有地區錄得暴風程度的陣風。讀者宜留意天氣變化，並特別提防與強雷雨帶相關的猛烈陣風。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在上午10時正發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

位於珠江口至深圳一帶的強雷雨帶即將由西至東橫過本港，本港西北將首當其衝。
強雷雨帶會為本港帶來猛烈陣風，有機會達颶風程度。此外，會有短時大驟雨及雷暴。在上午10時左右，流浮山錄得達颶風程度、每小時128公里的猛烈陣風。
正在戶外的讀者應到室內地方暫避。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在上午11時正取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

強雷雨帶正向東橫過珠江口，惟主體偏北，預料短期內可能影響新界部分地區，屆時該區天氣將再度轉差，可能伴隨強陣風、大驟雨及雷暴。
正在新界的讀者應保持警覺，留意最新天氣變化。

---

本港今早受強雷雨帶影響，天氣不穩。在黃色暴雨警告生效期間，流浮山一度錄得颶風程度的陣風。今早為何會出現「風雨交加」的現象？
出現猛烈陣風的主因，是強雷雨區相關的下沉氣流。強雷雨區一般伴隨較強的上升氣流，可以將雨水「撐在半空」。然而，今早的強雷雨帶靠近本港時稍作減弱。在上升氣流減弱的情況下，雨水就會因「承托力不足」而向下墜，順勢將大空高層的空氣「扯落地面」。
另一方面，今早的探空資料顯示，本港上空大氣中低層空氣較乾。這會加大氣流「扯落地面」的力度，而當氣流著地時便會形成突如其來猛烈的陣風。
強雷雨帶往往容易伴隨突如其來的猛烈陣風，對戶外活動或工作的人士構成風險。讀者可重溫MET WARN 早前製作的《氣象通識》，了解猛烈陣風的形成機制及應對措施。
此外，預料未來一星期本港仍然間中有雷雨，讀者應留意最新天氣消息。
黃雨唔只水浸咁簡單？點解暴雨有時伴隨猛烈陣風？ | MET WARN 氣象通識
https://youtu.be/F09-ZbzEKM4?si=MgjXR6xz7ox9eL8i
